title: Family members list — manual UAT/regression
prerequisites: Safety badge states and artwork must be confirmed against the product/design table referenced in Jira KUDO-35. Until that matrix is attached to the ticket or spec, badge-specific cases below are authored with explicit TBD state names—do not sign off badge visuals without that alignment.

Firebase Remote Config keys for family list refresh interval, low-battery threshold, stayed-place minutes, and walk/vehicle hysteresis must be identifiable in the RC console (or documented) for the RC matrix cases.

Health data requires Apple Health (iOS) or Health Connect (Android) test fixtures and members in known movement/location states. Use a staging build with RC overrides where production RC cannot be changed.

test_cases.title: All members Safe — alphabetical with You last
test_cases.preconditions: Family has three or more members; all are in Safe state; display names are distinct.
steps: Open the family members list screen.
Record the on-screen order of rows by display name (excluding grouping headers if any).
Identify the row labeled as the current user (“You”).
test_cases.expected_result: Non-You members appear in a single group sorted A→Z by display name (locale-aware per product). The “You” row is the last row in the list regardless of whether “You” would sort earlier alphabetically.

test_cases.title: All members Not safe — alphabetical with You last
test_cases.preconditions: All members including You are in Not safe state (per badge rules once design table is fixed).
steps: Open the family members list.
Note order of all rows.
test_cases.expected_result: All members sort A→Z by display name within the Not safe group; “You” remains the bottom row even if Not safe.

test_cases.title: Mixed Safe and Not safe — Not safe above Safe, You last
test_cases.preconditions: At least two Not safe and two Safe members; “You” is Safe.
steps: Open the family members list.
Split mentally into contiguous blocks: Not safe block, then Safe block.
test_cases.expected_result: Every Not safe row appears above every Safe row. Within Not safe: A→Z. Within Safe: A→Z. “You” is last overall.

test_cases.title: Mixed — “You” is Not safe — still last
test_cases.preconditions: “You” is Not safe; other members mix of Safe/Not safe.
steps: Open the family members list.
Confirm position of “You” relative to other Not safe names.
test_cases.expected_result: “You” remains the final row; other Not safe members sort A→Z above Safe group; Safe group A→Z.

test_cases.title: Duplicate-style display names — stable tie-break
test_cases.preconditions: Two different members share the same display name string (or differ only by trimming); product defines tie-break (e.g. internal id)—document observed rule.
steps: Open the list with both members in the same safety group.
Note relative order.
test_cases.expected_result: Order is deterministic (same on reload within same data). A→Z still holds; for identical sort keys, implementation-defined secondary order is consistent. Log actual rule for regression.

test_cases.title: Special characters and unicode in display names
test_cases.preconditions: Members with names including emoji, hyphen, apostrophe, accented characters; same safety grouping.
steps: Open the list.
Compare order to product-defined collation (locale).
test_cases.expected_result: Sorting matches documented collation for the app store region; no crash; “You” still last.

test_cases.title: Single member — only You
test_cases.preconditions: Family contains only the current user.
steps: Open the family members list.
test_cases.expected_result: One row for “You”; no ordering regressions or empty states incorrectly shown.

test_cases.title: Foreground periodic reload uses RC default interval (5 s)
test_cases.preconditions: RC key for list refresh interval reset to default (expected default 5 seconds) or fresh install; app foregrounded on list screen; tooling to observe network refresh or UI timestamp if exposed (otherwise use screen recording).
steps: Stay on the family members list with the app in foreground.
Change a server-side or mock-controlled attribute that affects ordering or row data (or use test harness).
Measure time until list reflects change without leaving the screen.
test_cases.expected_result: List picks up new ordering/data within approximately the default interval (5 s), subject to network; no manual navigation required while foregrounded.

test_cases.title: Foreground reload respects overridden RC interval
test_cases.preconditions: RC interval changed to a different value (e.g. 15 s); same observability as REL-001.
steps: Trigger a data change that affects the list.
Measure refresh latency on foreground list.
test_cases.expected_result: Refresh cadence matches overridden RC value within reasonable tolerance; not stuck on 5 s if RC says otherwise.

test_cases.title: Return from background reloads order and dependent row data
test_cases.preconditions: App backgrounded; meanwhile member safety or names change (fixture or second device).
steps: Open list, note order.
Background the app; apply change; return to foreground on same screen or navigate back to list.
Compare list to expected.
test_cases.expected_result: Order and dependent fields reload when returning from background without requiring a full app kill.

test_cases.title: Foreground refresh does not break scroll or selection expectations
test_cases.preconditions: List scrollable; if the product defines row selection/focus, enable that state.
steps: Scroll mid-list.
Wait through at least one periodic refresh.
If selection exists, observe whether spec requires preserve or reset—verify against product.
test_cases.expected_result: Behavior matches product spec (e.g. scroll position preserved or intentionally reset); no jarring jump, duplicated rows, or stuck loading state. File defect if spec silent and UX is unusable.

test_cases.title: Avatar always present on each row
test_cases.preconditions: Multiple members visible.
steps: Inspect each row on the family members list.
test_cases.expected_result: Every row shows an avatar (image or approved placeholder); no blank avatar area unless explicitly specified for pending invites (if applicable).

test_cases.title: Name single line with end ellipsis when long
test_cases.preconditions: A member with a very long display name.
steps: Open the list; locate the long name row.
Observe text layout.
test_cases.expected_result: Name occupies at most one line; overflow is trimmed with ellipsis (or equivalent) per platform; text does not wrap to a second line.

test_cases.title: Negative — name must not wrap to multiple lines
test_cases.preconditions: Extremely long single-token name.
steps: View the row.
test_cases.expected_result: No second line of name text; no full text expansion in the list row without explicit user action (if product forbids).

test_cases.title: Prerequisite — confirm badge state matrix with design
test_cases.preconditions: Design/product table from KUDO-35 available.
steps: Map each badge state from the table to a live or mock member state.
For each state, capture screenshot and label.
test_cases.expected_result: Authoritative list of states (e.g. Safe / Not safe / unknown) and assets is agreed; subsequent SAF cases use those exact labels.

test_cases.title: Badge state TBD-A — visual and semantics (replace label when matrix fixed)
test_cases.preconditions: Member forced to first badge state from design table (TBD—rename case when matrix attached).
steps: Open list; inspect badge on the row.
test_cases.expected_result: Badge matches design for state TBD-A (color, icon, label). Blocking until matrix exists.

test_cases.title: Badge state TBD-B — visual and semantics
test_cases.preconditions: Member forced to second badge state from design table.
steps: Inspect row badge.
test_cases.expected_result: Matches design for TBD-B.

test_cases.title: Badge state TBD-C — repeat for each additional table row
test_cases.preconditions: Duplicate this case in test management for every remaining row in the design table once known.
steps: Force state; verify badge.
test_cases.expected_result: One passing verification per design row; no undefined states shipped.

test_cases.title: Transition Safe → Not safe updates badge without stale delay beyond spec
test_cases.preconditions: Controllable fixture toggling safety.
steps: Start Safe; open list.
Toggle to Not safe; wait for list refresh cycle or foreground refresh.
Observe badge and list ordering.
test_cases.expected_result: Badge shows Not safe; member moves to Not safe ordering group when refresh applies; no indefinite stale Safe badge.

test_cases.title: Transition Not safe → Safe
test_cases.preconditions: Inverse of SAF-TR-001.
steps: Toggle Not safe to Safe; observe after refresh.
test_cases.expected_result: Badge Safe; member sorts into Safe group per ordering rules.

test_cases.title: Speed shown only when member In a vehicle
test_cases.preconditions: Member GPS indicates speed > 6 km/h with hysteresis satisfied (see RC default 6 s in hysteresis cases).
steps: Open list; locate member in vehicle state.
test_cases.expected_result: Speed value visible on row (unit per product); consistent with movement state.

test_cases.title: Negative — walking (1–6 km/h) hides speed
test_cases.preconditions: Member walking per GPS-derived speed band; not in vehicle.
steps: View row.
test_cases.expected_result: Speed is not shown.

test_cases.title: Negative — stationary / no movement hides speed
test_cases.preconditions: Member stationary.
steps: View row.
test_cases.expected_result: Speed hidden.

test_cases.title: Negative — no GPS / no location hides speed
test_cases.preconditions: Location unavailable for member.
steps: View row.
test_cases.expected_result: Speed hidden; activity may show “no location” per activity rules—speed still absent.

test_cases.title: Low battery label exact copy “Low battery” under default RC threshold
test_cases.preconditions: RC low-battery threshold at default 10%; member device battery < 10% (or mocked).
steps: Open list; read low-battery indicator text if shown.
test_cases.expected_result: Indicator appears; visible label text is exactly “Low battery” (case and spacing per final copy deck).

test_cases.title: Boundary — at threshold 10% (inclusive/exclusive per implementation)
test_cases.preconditions: Ability to set battery to exactly 10% or 9.9%.
steps: At 10%, note indicator presence.
At just below 10%, note presence.
test_cases.expected_result: Behavior matches implementation doc; document inclusive/exclusive rule for regression.

test_cases.title: Negative — battery at or above threshold does not show indicator
test_cases.preconditions: Battery ≥ threshold (e.g. 11% with default 10%).
steps: View row.
test_cases.expected_result: No “Low battery” indicator.

test_cases.title: RC overrides low-battery threshold
test_cases.preconditions: RC threshold changed (e.g. 20%); battery set between old and new thresholds.
steps: Verify indicator appears only when below new threshold.
test_cases.expected_result: RC value drives visibility; default remains 10% when RC absent.

test_cases.title: Low battery yes/no feeds activity status consistently
test_cases.preconditions: Member online; other activity fragments present.
steps: With low battery, read full activity text.
With sufficient battery, read activity text.
test_cases.expected_result: Activity composition reflects low battery yes/no per spec; no contradiction with row-level low-battery chip.

test_cases.title: Negative — all health indicators hidden when In a vehicle
test_cases.preconditions: Member in vehicle state; health data exists for today.
steps: View row.
test_cases.expected_result: No Steps, Sleep, Heart rate, Blood pressure, or Blood sugar summary lines on the row.

test_cases.title: Today metrics use viewing member local timezone for “today” boundary
test_cases.preconditions: Viewer timezone differs from data source day boundary scenario (fixture); health events near midnight.
steps: Compare which readings appear vs viewer’s local calendar day.
test_cases.expected_result: Only metrics belonging to “today” in the viewing member’s local timezone appear (per spec); document edge case at day boundary.

test_cases.title: Interpretation A — at most two distinct health indicator types shown
test_cases.preconditions: PM selects interpretation A: “up to closest 2 indicators” read as at most two metric types. Fresh values for 3+ types today.
steps: Open row; count distinct indicator types displayed.
test_cases.expected_result: No more than two indicator types visible; chosen types are the “closest” per product definition of recency.

test_cases.title: Interpretation B — latest three readings drawn from two indicator types
test_cases.preconditions: PM selects interpretation B: “latest 3 values from 2 indicators”. Fixture with multiple samples per type.
steps: Verify which values appear and that they are the latest three datapoints spanning at most two types.
test_cases.expected_result: UI matches interpretation B; if PM leaves A, mark this case N/A.

test_cases.title: Tie-break when timestamps equal — Blood pressure before Blood sugar before Sleep before Heart rate before Steps
test_cases.preconditions: Synthetic or logged data with identical timestamps to the owner for multiple types.
steps: Observe which two types win when only two may show (or ordering under interpretation B).
test_cases.expected_result: Priority order matches: Blood pressure → Blood sugar → Sleep → Heart rate → Steps.

test_cases.title: Steps format matches example (e.g. 1,843)
test_cases.preconditions: Known step count.
steps: Compare displayed Steps string to locale thousands separator rules.
test_cases.expected_result: Format like `1,843` for en-US style; matches final copy guide.

test_cases.title: Sleep format matches example (e.g. 5hr34m)
test_cases.preconditions: Known sleep duration.
steps: Read Sleep field.
test_cases.expected_result: Format matches spec example; spacing and units per copy.

test_cases.title: Heart rate format (e.g. 87 BPM)
test_cases.preconditions: Known HR.
steps: Read Heart rate field.
test_cases.expected_result: Includes BPM (or localized equivalent) per final copy.

test_cases.title: Blood pressure format and units (verify mmHg vs ticket typo mmHG)
test_cases.preconditions: Known BP reading.
steps: Read Blood pressure display.
test_cases.expected_result: Matches approved copy (e.g. `123/85 mmHg`); ticket typo `mmHG` must not ship if copy uses correct casing.

test_cases.title: Blood sugar format (e.g. 6.6 mmol/L); ticket typo “Bloog” must not ship
test_cases.preconditions: Known glucose.
steps: Read Blood sugar display.
test_cases.expected_result: Correct spelling “Blood sugar”; value format `6.6 mmol/L` or localized unit per product.

test_cases.title: Cross-timezone — member vs viewer “today” if spec defines both
test_cases.preconditions: Spec clarifies whose timezone wins for shared data; fixture across zones.
steps: Compare displayed metrics to spec boundary definition.
test_cases.expected_result: Matches documented rule; if spec only says viewer TZ, align with HLT-002.

test_cases.title: Activity text max two lines; overflow trimmed
test_cases.preconditions: Member online with many simultaneous status factors (place + movement + phone + weather + time + connectivity + battery).
steps: View activity status on row.
test_cases.expected_result: At most two lines; ellipsis/trim without breaking layout.

test_cases.title: Place — at home / work / saved place when geofence matches
test_cases.preconditions: GPS/geofence places member in labeled zone.
steps: Read activity statement.
test_cases.expected_result: Contains correct place class per product strings (home/work/saved).

test_cases.title: Place — other location stayed before (stay ≥ RC minutes, default 20)
test_cases.preconditions: Prior visit exceeded stayed threshold; currently at that other location.
steps: Verify activity references “stayed before” semantics per copy.
test_cases.expected_result: Correct classification; threshold from RC (default 20 min).

test_cases.title: Place — other location never stayed before
test_cases.preconditions: First-time short visit or stay duration under threshold.
steps: Read activity.
test_cases.expected_result: Matches “never stayed” branch per copy.

test_cases.title: Movement — no GPS shows no-location branch
test_cases.preconditions: GPS absent.
steps: Read activity.
test_cases.expected_result: Reflects no location per formula; no vehicle speed line (see SPD cases).

test_cases.title: Precise location off vs on reflected in status
test_cases.preconditions: Toggle precise location permission.
steps: Compare activity wording for precise off vs on.
test_cases.expected_result: Matches product copy for reduced precision state.

test_cases.title: Walking band 1–6 km/h and vehicle >6 km/h with hysteresis
test_cases.preconditions: RC hysteresis default 6 s; ability to simulate speeds.
steps: Oscillate around 6 km/h boundary; observe how long state persists before flip.
test_cases.expected_result: State changes only after >6 s in new movement class (per spec); matches walk vs vehicle strings.

test_cases.title: Phone — in call state appears when on call
test_cases.preconditions: Member on active call.
steps: Read activity.
test_cases.expected_result: Call fragment present per copy.

test_cases.title: Phone — using phone (unlocked and/or touch)
test_cases.preconditions: Screen unlocked / touch events generating.
steps: Read activity.
test_cases.expected_result: Fragment appears per rules; does not falsely show when locked and idle if spec forbids.

test_cases.title: Phone stationary from motion sensor true/false
test_cases.preconditions: Device motion controllable.
steps: Compare activity when phone moving vs stationary.
test_cases.expected_result: Motion stationary flag influences statement as per product copy.

test_cases.title: Severe weather — heavy rain / snow / storm at member location
test_cases.preconditions: Weather mock or live severe condition.
steps: Read activity.
test_cases.expected_result: Weather fragment appears only for qualifying severities per product.

test_cases.title: Local late night 21:00–05:00 for the person
test_cases.preconditions: Device local time in window.
steps: Read activity.
test_cases.expected_result: Late-night fragment appears in window; absent outside.

test_cases.title: Online connectivity — non-offline activity may include connectivity fragment
test_cases.preconditions: Member online.
steps: Read activity for optional connectivity wording.
test_cases.expected_result: Matches spec for online (if no extra fragment, document).

test_cases.title: Offline — only “No connection, last seen …” family; last seen <1 min → just now
test_cases.preconditions: Member offline; last seen <1 minute ago.
steps: Read entire activity string.
test_cases.expected_result: Exactly the offline template; text includes “No connection, last seen just now”; no place/movement/weather/battery fragments.

test_cases.title: Offline — 1≤X<60 minutes → “No connection, last seen Xm ago”
test_cases.preconditions: Last seen in minute band.
steps: Verify X matches elapsed minutes (floor/round per implementation—document).
test_cases.expected_result: Only offline string family; correct minute wording.

test_cases.title: Offline — <24 h → “Yh ago” with Y = hours % 24 (validate vs app)
test_cases.preconditions: Last seen e.g. 25 h ago (ticket math ambiguous with “<24h” branch—exercise boundaries 23h, 24h, 25h, 47h).
steps: For each fixture, record displayed Y.
Compare to ticket formula and to sensible wall-clock hours.
test_cases.expected_result: Implementation is consistent and documented; escalate if ticket `% 24` conflicts with UX (e.g. 25h should not show as 1h without PM approval).

test_cases.title: Offline — <30 days → “Zd ago” with Z = days % 30 (validate edge cases)
test_cases.preconditions: Last seen spanning day buckets crossing 24h and 30d boundaries.
steps: Verify Z for known elapsed days.
Note ambiguity when ticket `% 30` conflicts with calendar clarity.
test_cases.expected_result: Matches shipping app behavior with written acceptance of formula vs user-understandable days.

test_cases.title: Offline — beyond threshold → “No connection long time ago”
test_cases.preconditions: Last seen >30 days or implementation upper bound.
steps: Read status.
test_cases.expected_result: Shows long-time string; no Xm/Yh/Zd variants incorrectly.

test_cases.title: Negative — offline row must not show speed or health summaries
test_cases.preconditions: Member offline; historical health exists.
steps: Inspect row.
test_cases.expected_result: No speed; no health chips (unless product explicitly allows—default expect hidden per list row rules).

test_cases.title: Negative — offline string contains no extra status fragments
test_cases.preconditions: Offline with rich prior context.
steps: Read full activity.
test_cases.expected_result: No home/work/walk/vehicle/weather fragments appended to offline template.

test_cases.title: RC — foreground list refresh interval default 5 s (single focused case)
test_cases.preconditions: RC at default; measurable list update.
steps: Confirm refresh cadence ≈5 s as in REL-001.
test_cases.expected_result: Passes default matrix cell for interval.

test_cases.title: RC — low battery threshold default 10%
test_cases.preconditions: RC at default.
steps: Exercise BAT-002 boundary at 10%.
test_cases.expected_result: Default threshold verified.

test_cases.title: RC — stayed-place minutes default 20
test_cases.preconditions: RC at default; visit duration controllable.
steps: Stay 19 min vs 21 min at novel place; compare ACT-003/004 outcomes.
test_cases.expected_result: 20-minute default drives stayed classification.

test_cases.title: RC — walk/vehicle hysteresis default 6 s
test_cases.preconditions: RC at default.
steps: Repeat ACT-007 focusing on 6 s requirement.
test_cases.expected_result: 6 s hysteresis honored from RC default.

test_cases.title: RC matrix — overridden values for all four keys in one session (if harness allows)
test_cases.preconditions: Staging RC or mock exposing all four parameters.
steps: Set non-default interval, battery threshold, stayed minutes, hysteresis.
Re-run condensed checks for each parameter.
test_cases.expected_result: Each non-default value is reflected in UI behavior; mark blocked if manual RC publish is not possible.

test_cases.title: Negative — no fabricated health when source has no today data
test_cases.preconditions: Health permissions on but empty today.
steps: View row (not in vehicle).
test_cases.expected_result: No placeholder fake metrics; empty state per design.

test_cases.title: Negative — speed hidden during walk even if GPS noisy spike
test_cases.preconditions: Simulate brief GPS spike above 6 km/h shorter than hysteresis while true state walking.
steps: Observe row during spike window.
test_cases.expected_result: Speed does not flash vehicle-only UI without sustained vehicle state.
